com.newland.nsdk.core.api.external.keymanager

Interface ExtKeyManager

  • All Superinterfaces:
    Module

    public interface ExtKeyManager
extends Module
    [External Module] Provides the ability to manage keys in the PIN pad.

    How to get this module:

         ExtKeyManager extKeyManager = (ExtKeyManager)ExtNSDKModuleManagerImpl.getInstance().getModule(ModuleType.EXT_KEY_MANAGER);

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void clearSymmetricKeys()
      Clears all symmetric keys in the device.
      void commitAtomic(boolean isSuccessful)
      Commits the result of remote key loading.
      void deleteKey(Key key)
      Deletes a specified key.
      void generateAsymKey(AsymmetricKey dstKey, AsymAlgInfo asymAlgInfo)
      Generates asymmetric key.
      void generateKey(KeyGenerateMethod method, AlgorithmParameters algorithmParameters, SymmetricKey srcKey, Key dstKey, byte[] additionalData)
      Generates symmetric/asymmetric key in PIN pad under the protection of specified symmetric key.
      byte[] generateKeyWithAsymKey(KeyGenerateMethod method, AsymAlgorithmParameters algorithmParameters, AsymmetricKey srcKey, SymmetricKey dstKey)
      Generates symmetric key in PIN pad under the protection of specified asymmetric key.
      byte[] getKeyInfo(KeyInfoID infoID, Key key)
      Gets key info.
      void increaseKSN(byte groupId)
      Increases DUKPT KSN.
      void initAtomic()
      Backs up keys.
      byte[] loadTrustedCert(boolean isCA, byte[] cert)
      Verifies Newland certificate and returns its public key.
      void resetCertStatus()
      Resets certificates that loaded before.

    • Method Detail

      • generateKey

        void generateKey(KeyGenerateMethod method,
                 AlgorithmParameters algorithmParameters,
                 SymmetricKey srcKey,
                 Key dstKey,
                 byte[] additionalData)
          throws NSDKException
        Generates symmetric/asymmetric key in PIN pad under the protection of specified symmetric key.

        Example:

             // Case 1: Load symmetric key
     SymmetricKey sourceKey = new SymmetricKey();
     SymmetricKey dstKey = new SymmetricKey();

     AlgorithmParameters algorithmParameters = new AlgorithmParameters();
     algorithmParameters.setCipherMode(CipherMode.ECB);

     sourceKey.setKeyID((byte)1);
     sourceKey.setKeyType(KeyType.DES);
     sourceKey.setKeyUsage(KeyUsage.KEK);

     dstKey.setKeyID((byte)2);
     dstKey.setKeyType(KeyType.DES);
     dstKey.setKeyUsage(KeyUsage.DATA);
     dstKey.setKeyLen(16);
     dstKey.setKeyData(ISOUtils.hex2byte("253C9D9D7C2FBBFA253C9D9D7C2FBBFA"));

     try {
         extKeyManager.generateKey(KeyGenerateMethod.CIPHER, algorithmParameters, sourceKey, dstKey);
     } catch (NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the exception
     }

     // Case 2: Load asymmetric key
     String certEnc = FileUtils.readFromAssets(context, "kms_enc.pem");
     byte[] certEncBuf = certEnc.getBytes();
     byte[] keyData;

     try {
         keyData = keyManager.loadTrustedCert(false, certEncBuf);
     } catch (NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the error
         return;
     }

     AsymmetricKey distributionKey = new AsymmetricKey();
     distributionKey.setKeyType(AsymKeyType.RSA);
     distributionKey.setKeyUsage(AsymKeyUsage.KEY_DISTRIBUTION);
     distributionKey.setKeyID((byte)3);
     distributionKey.setKeyData(keyData);
     distributionKey.setKeyLen(keyData.length);

     try {
         extKeyManager.generateKey(KeyGenerateMethod.CLEAR, null, null, distributionKey, certEncBuf);
     } catch (NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the error
     }
        Parameters:
        method - [Required] Key injection method， see KeyGenerateMethod.
        • For most cases, set this to KeyGenerateMethod.CIPHER
        • For TR31 keys, set this to KeyGenerateMethod.TR31
        • For Giske keys, set this to KeyGenerateMethod.GISKE
        algorithmParameters - [Optional] Algorithm parameters, used when external device based on Forth firmware. See AlgorithmParameters
        • Default cipher mode: CipherMode.ECB
        • Default padding mode: PaddingMode.NONE
        • IV is required when cipher mode is CipherMode.CBC
        srcKey - [Required] The key to protect the target key.
        • For external device based on Forth firmware, the following parameters are required:
          • Key index
          • Key type
          • Key usage
        • Otherwise, only key index is required.
        dstKey - [Required] Target key to be generated in PIN pad. The following parameters are required:
        • Key index
        • Key usage
        • Key type
        • Key data
        • Key length
        • KSN(Required if it is a DUKPT key)
        • KCV(Optional)
        additionalData - [Optional] Additional data. Usually when target key is asymmetric key, use this parameter to set certificate data.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • generateKeyWithAsymKey

        byte[] generateKeyWithAsymKey(KeyGenerateMethod method,
                              AsymAlgorithmParameters algorithmParameters,
                              AsymmetricKey srcKey,
                              SymmetricKey dstKey)
                       throws NSDKException
        Generates symmetric key in PIN pad under the protection of specified asymmetric key.

        Example:

             AsymmetricKey sourceKey = new SymmetricKey();
     SymmetricKey dstKey = new SymmetricKey();

     AsymAlgorithmParameters algorithmParameters = new AsymAlgorithmParameters();
     algorithmParameters.setMessageDigestType(MessageDigestType.SHA256);
     algorithmParameters.setEncodingMode(AsymEncodingMode.PKCS_V15);

     sourceKey.setKeyID(1);
     sourceKey.setKeyType(AsymKeyType.RSA);
     sourceKey.setKeyUsage(AsymKeyUsage.KEY_DISTRIBUTION);

     dstKey.setKeyID(2);
     dstKey.setKeyType(KeyType.DES);
     dstKey.setKeyUsage(KeyUsage.TR31_KEK);
     dstKey.setKeyLen(24);

     try {
         byte[] keyData = extKeyManager.generateKey(KeyGenerateMethod.RANDOM_OUT, algorithmParameters, sourceKey, dstKey);
     } catch (NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the exception
     }
        Parameters:
        method - [Required] Key injection method. For now, only KeyGenerateMethod.CIPHER and KeyGenerateMethod.RANDOM_OUT are supported.
        algorithmParameters - Algorithm parameters, see AlgorithmParameters
        • [Required] Message digest type
        • [Required] Encoding mode
        srcKey - [Required] The key to protect the target key.
        • Default key type: AsymKeyType.RSA
        • Default key usage: AsymKeyUsage.KEY_DISTRIBUTION
        dstKey - [Required] Target key be generated in PIN pad.
        • Default key type: KeyType.DES
        • Default key usage: KeyUsage.KEK
        • Default KCV mode: KCVMode.NONE
        Returns:
        When method is KeyGenerateMethod.RANDOM_OUT, random key will be generated and returned.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • generateAsymKey

        void generateAsymKey(AsymmetricKey dstKey,
                     AsymAlgInfo asymAlgInfo)
              throws NSDKException
        Generates asymmetric key.

        For example:

             AsymmetricKey asymmetricKey = new AsymmetricKey();
     asymmetricKey.setKeyID((byte) 249);
     asymmetricKey.setKeyType(AsymKeyType.RSA);
     asymmetricKey.setKeyUsage(AsymKeyUsage.KEY_DISTRIBUTION);
     AsymAlgInfo algInfo = new AsymAlgInfo();
     algInfo.setUnBit(2048);
     algInfo.setUcRSAPubExp(new byte[] {0x01, 0x00, 0x01});

     try {
         extKeyManager.generateAsymKey(asymmetricKey, algInfo);
     } catch (NSDKException e) {
         //Handle an exception
     }
        Parameters:
        dstKey - [Required] Target asymmetric key to be injected in PIN pad, see AsymmetricKey.
        asymAlgInfo - [Required] The asymmetric algorithm information, see AsymAlgInfo.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • getKeyInfo

        byte[] getKeyInfo(KeyInfoID infoID,
                  Key key)
           throws NSDKException
        Gets key info.

        Example:

             // Get KSN
     DUKPTKey infoKey = new DUKPTKey();
     infoKey.setKeyID((byte)2);
     infoKey.setKeyType(KeyType.DES);
     infoKey.setKeyUsage(KeyUsage.DUKPT);
     try {
         byte[] ksn = extKeyManager.getKeyInfo(KeyInfoID.KSN, infoKey);
     } catch (NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the exception
     }

     // Get KCV
     SymmetricKey desKey = new SymmetricKey();
     desKey.setKeyID((byte)2);
     desKey.setKeyType(KeyType.DES);
     desKey.setKeyUsage(KeyUsage.DATA);
     try {
         byte[] kcv = extKeyManager.getKeyInfo(KeyInfoID.KCV, desKey);
     } catch (NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the exception
     }
        Parameters:
        infoID - [Required] What info to get. See KeyInfoID.
        key - [Required] Which key's info to get.
        Returns:
        Key info.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • increaseKSN

        void increaseKSN(byte groupId)
          throws NSDKException
        Increases DUKPT KSN.
        Parameters:
        groupId - [Required] DUKPT group id. Value range: [1-250].
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • deleteKey

        void deleteKey(Key key)
        throws NSDKException
        Deletes a specified key.

        Example:

             SymmetricKey delKey = new SymmetricKey();
     delKey.setKeyID((byte)2);
     delKey.setKeyType(KeyType.DES);
     delKey.setKeyUsage(KeyUsage.PIN);
     try {
         extKeyManager.deleteKey(delKey);
     } catch (NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the exception
     }
        Parameters:
        key - [Required] Which key to delete.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • loadTrustedCert

        byte[] loadTrustedCert(boolean isCA,
                       byte[] cert)
                throws NSDKException
        Verifies Newland certificate and returns its public key.

        Note: This is usually used to verify Newland certificates of KDH(Key Distribution Host) during RKI(Remote Key Injection) process.

        Parameters:
        isCA - [Required] Indicates if the certificate is CA.
        cert - [Required] Newland certificate data.
        Returns:
        The public key of the certificate.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • resetCertStatus

        void resetCertStatus()
              throws NSDKException
        Resets certificates that loaded before.
          Note:
        • This is used to clear loaded KDH(Key Distribution Host) certificates before starting RKI(Remote Key Injection) process.
        • This shall be called before loadTrustedCert(boolean, byte[])
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • initAtomic

        void initAtomic()
         throws NSDKException
        Backs up keys.

        This is usually called before loading keys and work with commitAtomic(boolean) to ensure that either all keys loaded, or no keys loaded.

        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • commitAtomic

        void commitAtomic(boolean isSuccessful)
           throws NSDKException
        Commits the result of remote key loading.
        Parameters:
        isSuccessful - [Required] The result of remote key loading.
        • true: All the keys are loaded successfully.
        • false: Error occurred during key loading, all of the keys that already loaded will be reversed.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • clearSymmetricKeys

        void clearSymmetricKeys()
                 throws NSDKException
        Clears all symmetric keys in the device.
        Throws:
        NSDKException
